META.reasoning: MACRO REGIME: Mixed-to-cautious. Fed held at 3.5%-3.75% (third consecutive hold), higher-for-longer confirmed with no cuts priced through 2026. US-Iran military blockade is the dominant left-tail geopolitical risk driving energy inflation. The regime wiki explicitly flags Meta's own 9% capex shock drop as 'injecting sector uncertainty' — this is not just a stock-specific event but a sector-wide read-through for AI capex sustainability. The regime is transitional for communication services / AI infrastructure names. RELATIVE STRENGTH: META is materially underperforming the broader market. The S&P 500 is up ~9% for April (tracking best month since 2020), while META has suffered a significant earnings-driven selloff. Current price ~603 vs daily close of 669 on 2026-04-29 represents an approximately 10% intraday/afterhours collapse. Hourly indicators confirm extreme weakness: price is 9.5%-10% below all hourly EMAs (24h, 48h, 120h), hourly RSI(21) at 14.7 (deeply oversold), hourly minus_di at 55.14 vs plus_di at 8.27 (heavy bearish momentum), and hourly OBV trending down. Volume at 9.99x the 20-period hourly average confirms institutional distribution, not accumulation. META is a clear sector laggard in this session. ASYMMETRY CHECK: Potential entry near current ~603. To the upside, nearest daily support-turned-resistance at 627.61 (~4.1% up) and then 651 Fib 23.6% level (~8% up). To the downside, the next daily pivot low is 520.26 (~13.7% down); intermediate Fib extension 1272 low-extension at 579 offers ~4% downside. For a long entry: upside to 627 is ~4%, downside risk to 579 is ~4%, giving 1:1 R/R — well below the 2:1 minimum threshold. A stretched target to 651 (Fib 23.6%) yields ~8% upside vs ~4% downside = 2:1, but this requires the capex shock thesis to fully reverse, which is speculative within 2-20 days. CAPITAL PRESERVATION FLAGS: The wiki thesis TL;DR confirms the bear case is 'capex is value-destructive' — this quarter's capex shock ($115-135B plan) is the materialization of that bear case risk, not a new hypothesis. With the macro regime cautious and META now having triggered its own sector uncertainty event, adding a position here carries elevated thesis-violation risk. Hourly z-score at -1.69 confirms the price is 1.7 standard deviations below the 120-period hourly mean — extreme but not at a level that guarantees bounce. CONCLUSION: The macro regime is risk-off for META specifically (capex shock is a thesis-changing event per regime wiki). The R/R for a fresh long does not clear 2:1 on realistic near-term targets. The stock is not suitable for a fresh short either given it is already -10% on the session with RSI(21h) at 14.7. The macro signal (capex bear case materializing) directly contradicts any clean bullish technical thesis. Passing on this setup pending stabilization confirmation and reassessment of the capex narrative over 3-5 days.
